steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to indicate how Fulton, Missouri, got its name. The answer must (a) name Fulton, Missouri and (b) state who or what it was named after.

steps.title: Find the choice that does both
steps.content: The sentence “Fulton, Missouri, is named after Robert Fulton, designer of the first commercially successful steamboat” names the city and identifies Robert Fulton as its namesake, with a brief tag about who he was.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Fulton, Missouri, shares its name with Fulton Street in New York City.’: notes both are called Fulton but never says where the Missouri city's name came from — misses the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Fulton Street is in New York City, where the steamboat Clermont was designed in 1807.’: about Fulton Street and the Clermont, not about how the Missouri city got its name. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Designed in 1807 in New York City, Clermont was the first commercially successful steamboat.’: entirely about the Clermont; Fulton, Missouri is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Fulton, Missouri, is named after Robert Fulton, designer of the first commercially successful steamboat.’: names the city, identifies the namesake, and adds why he is notable. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
